message:
fix(compat): reject malformed noop batches and invalid open flag mixes

The compat surface had two correctness gaps that let sqlite-style callers get
behavior that drifted from the contract they were asking for.

First, execute_batch() treated any whitespace/comment-only input as a no-op,
but the block-comment scanner silently accepted unterminated /* ... input. That
meant a malformed SQL batch could be reported as a successful empty batch
instead of surfacing a parse error. Make batch_is_noop() return Result<bool,
FrankenError>, preserve the fast no-op path for real comment-only batches, and
raise ParseError when a block comment never closes. Add focused regressions for
both the helper and execute_batch().

Second, open_with_flags() previously collapsed sqlite3_open_v2-style access
modes too aggressively. A READ_WRITE open without CREATE would create missing
databases anyway, READ_ONLY | CREATE was not rejected, CREATE by itself was not
rejected, and READ_ONLY :memory: accidentally returned a writable connection.
Tighten the compat mapping so we classify access modes explicitly, reject the
invalid combinations, fail READ_WRITE opens on missing files unless CREATE is
also present, reject read-only in-memory opens, and prefer READONLY over
READWRITE when both bits are present in the VFS flag projection.

Also move fsqlite-parser into the main dependency set so the crate manifest
stays aligned with the current public-facade layout instead of relying on a
dev-dependency slot.
